title: CSS: Responsive and Accessible Styling
summary: A modern CSS course covering cascade, selectors, sizing, typography, color, normal flow, Flexbox, Grid, responsive design, reusable systems, motion and accessibility.

assumed_knowledge: HTML course or equivalent semantic markup diagnostic
audience.target_capability: Independently design, implement and audit a responsive multi-page visual system that preserves semantic HTML, keyboard visibility, readability and user preferences.

includes: CSS syntax, cascade, inheritance and specificity
Custom properties, layers and component organization
Cross-browser debugging and accessible visual review
non_goals: Advanced Houdini or custom paint APIs
Replacing semantic structure with visual ordering

runtime.standard: CSS Snapshot 2024 modules used by current evergreen browsers
runtime.execution_environment: Sandboxed browser preview with reference HTML fixtures and deterministic local assets
notes: Experimental features require explicit compatibility labels.
Source order must remain meaningful when visual layout changes.

modules.title: Syntax and cascade
modules.description: Write rules and predict which declarations become computed values.

skills.title: Rules, declarations and values
skills.description: Write selectors and property-value declarations with valid syntax and units.
outcomes: Author and debug a stylesheet containing grouped rules and shorthand or longhand declarations.

skills.title: Origins, importance and layers
skills.description: Predict precedence across user-agent, user and author origins, importance and cascade layers.
outcomes: Determine the winning declaration in a layered multi-origin example.

skills.title: Specificity and source order
skills.description: Calculate selector specificity and prefer maintainable ordering over escalation.
outcomes: Explain and repair a specificity conflict without adding unnecessary important declarations.

skills.title: Inheritance, initial and computed values
skills.description: Distinguish inherited, initial, unset and computed behavior including custom properties.
outcomes: Trace a property's specified and computed value through an element tree.

modules.title: Selectors and reusable systems
modules.description: Target semantic structures and organize predictable reusable styles.

skills.title: Type, class, attribute and combinator selectors
skills.description: Select elements by stable semantic hooks and structural relationships.
outcomes: Choose selectors that are specific enough without coupling to incidental nesting.

skills.title: Pseudo-classes and pseudo-elements
skills.description: Style interaction, structural and generated parts while preserving native behavior.
outcomes: Implement hover, focus-visible, validation and structural states without hiding keyboard focus.

skills.title: Custom properties and design tokens
skills.description: Define inheriting reusable values with local overrides and safe fallbacks.
outcomes: Create a small color, spacing and typography token layer and trace an override.

skills.title: Stylesheet organization
skills.description: Separate reset/base, layout, component and utility concerns with documented layering and naming.
outcomes: Refactor a conflicting stylesheet into a predictable layered structure.

modules.title: Box model, sizing and overflow
modules.description: Reason precisely about dimensions, spacing and content that exceeds available space.

skills.title: Content, padding, border and margin
skills.description: Calculate used box dimensions and apply border-box sizing deliberately.
outcomes: Trace and repair unexpected element size using the browser box-model inspector.

skills.title: Lengths, percentages and viewport units
skills.description: Choose relative or absolute units based on typography, container and viewport relationships.
outcomes: Select and justify units for readable text, spacing and responsive dimensions.

skills.title: Intrinsic sizing and constraints
skills.description: Use min/max/content-based sizing and aspect ratio to avoid brittle fixed dimensions.
outcomes: Implement a component that grows, shrinks and wraps without clipping realistic content.

skills.title: Overflow and containment
skills.description: Handle long words, scroll regions and clipping without hiding essential content or focus.
outcomes: Repair overflow across narrow, zoomed and long-content scenarios.

modules.title: Typography, color and visual treatment
modules.description: Create readable hierarchy and sufficient contrast across user settings.

skills.title: Font families and loading
skills.description: Define resilient font stacks and load web fonts without unreadable fallback assumptions.
outcomes: Configure a font stack and explain fallback, loading and privacy trade-offs.

skills.title: Readable text metrics
skills.description: Set scalable size, line height, measure, weight, alignment and spacing without blocking text zoom.
outcomes: Create and audit readable body, heading and code typography at 200 percent zoom.

skills.title: Color and contrast
skills.description: Choose foreground, background, focus and state colors that satisfy contrast and do not encode meaning alone.
outcomes: Measure and repair text, component and focus-indicator contrast.

skills.title: Borders, shadows, gradients and backgrounds
skills.description: Use decorative layers without obscuring content, focus or contrast.
outcomes: Implement a visual surface treatment that degrades safely and preserves readability.

modules.title: Flow, positioning and stacking
modules.description: Understand normal layout before applying explicit positioning and layering.

skills.title: Normal flow and display
skills.description: Reason about block, inline and flow-root participation before choosing a layout system.
outcomes: Predict and repair layout using normal flow and appropriate display behavior.

skills.title: Relative, absolute, fixed and sticky positioning
skills.description: Choose positioned layout with correct containing blocks and document-flow consequences.
outcomes: Implement and debug a positioned component without covering content or focus targets.

skills.title: Stacking contexts and z-order
skills.description: Explain paint order and stacking-context boundaries instead of escalating z-index values blindly.
outcomes: Diagnose and repair an overlay ordering defect using stacking-context evidence.

skills.title: Logical properties and writing modes
skills.description: Use block/inline-relative sizing and spacing for direction- and language-resilient layouts.
outcomes: Refactor directional properties and verify a component in left-to-right and right-to-left contexts.

modules.title: Flexbox and Grid
modules.description: Build one- and two-dimensional layouts without reordering semantic meaning.

skills.title: Flex axes and sizing
skills.description: Use flex containers, direction, basis, growth, shrinkage and wrapping deliberately.
outcomes: Implement and explain a resilient one-dimensional component layout.

skills.title: Flex alignment and order
skills.description: Align items and groups while avoiding visual order that contradicts DOM and keyboard order.
outcomes: Debug alignment and verify meaningful source and focus order.

skills.title: Grid tracks and placement
skills.description: Define explicit and implicit tracks with repeat, minmax, lines and named areas.
outcomes: Implement and inspect a two-dimensional page or component grid.

skills.title: Intrinsic responsive grids
skills.description: Combine flexible tracks, auto placement and content constraints before adding breakpoints.
outcomes: Create a grid that adapts across content and container widths without fixed device assumptions.

modules.title: Responsive design and motion
modules.description: Adapt to containers, devices and user preferences without breaking content.

skills.title: Media and feature queries
skills.description: Add content-driven breakpoints and feature or preference queries as progressive enhancements.
outcomes: Implement and test layout, color-scheme and contrast adaptations.

skills.title: Container queries
skills.description: Adapt reusable components to their available container rather than only the viewport.
outcomes: Build and explain a component with a size-based container query and safe baseline.

skills.title: Transforms, transitions and animations
skills.description: Animate composite-friendly properties for feedback without making content inaccessible or disorienting.
outcomes: Implement purposeful state motion and explain its start, end and interruption behavior.

skills.title: Reduced motion and input preferences
skills.description: Respect reduced-motion and other user preferences with equivalent non-motion feedback.
outcomes: Verify and repair an experience under reduced-motion, zoom and keyboard-only settings.

modules.title: Debugging, compatibility and project
modules.description: Deliver a maintainable responsive visual system with verification evidence.

skills.title: Computed styles and layout inspectors
skills.description: Use matched rules, computed values, box, flex and grid inspectors to find the actual cause of a defect.
outcomes: Diagnose a cascade and a layout failure using recorded developer-tool evidence.

skills.title: Compatibility and progressive enhancement
skills.description: Check support and supply a usable baseline before optional newer features.
outcomes: Implement and test a fallback for a feature whose support is not universal in the target matrix.

skills.title: Visual accessibility audit
skills.description: Review contrast, focus visibility, reflow, zoom, text spacing, target size and motion preferences.
outcomes: Record and repair findings from a WCAG-informed visual and interaction audit.

skills.title: Responsive site design system
skills.description: Integrate tokens, layers, typography, layout, components, states and preferences across a multi-page site.
outcomes: Deliver and explain a responsive, accessible and maintainable CSS system with documented compatibility targets.

exit_outcomes: Predict and debug the cascade, inheritance, box model and layout using browser evidence.
Build responsive components and page layouts with normal flow, Flexbox, Grid and content-driven queries.
Create a reusable token and layer system without excessive specificity or semantic source-order changes.
Verify contrast, focus visibility, reflow, zoom, text spacing and reduced-motion behavior across the declared target matrix.
